L1.area: Data stream decompression and decoding pipeline

L2.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in the decompression stream reading logic where an unsafe block uses uninitialized memory as a temporary placeholder during an internal state swap. If a panic occurs between placing the uninitialized value and restoring valid state, the uninitialized memory may be dropped or read, leading to arbitrary code execution.

L3.area: Gzip format multi-stream decompression and decoder state management
L3.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in the gzip decompression stream reading logic where an unsafe block uses uninitialized memory as a temporary placeholder during the internal state swap of a chained stream decoder. If a panic occurs between placing the uninitialized value and restoring valid state, the uninitialized memory may be dropped or read, leading to arbitrary code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The MultiDecoder's read implementation uses mem::replace with mem::uninitialized() to temporarily move the decoder state, creating a use-after-free if a panic occurs before valid state is restored.
